name: armasai-adl-onehanded
description: Egocentric ADL clips of a single left-hand-absent subject performing bimanual tasks one-handed. Ground-truth labels for the perception agent: the specific action, which side needs the prosthesis (affected), the compensating side (residual), and the ADL task categories the design should optimize.
subject: single subject, left upper limb absent

label_schema.file: path to clip relative to repo root
label_schema.primary_action: specific human-written description of the main action
label_schema.affected_side: left | right — limb that needs the prosthesis
label_schema.tasks: subset of [reach, grasp, feeding] the action implies
label_schema.bimanual_normally: true if the task normally needs two hands

videos.primary_action: opening a bottle cap one-handed, then picking up the bottle
videos.notes: twisting a cap normally needs one hand to hold the bottle and the other to twist; done here single-handed, then the bottle is lifted off the surface

videos.primary_action: tearing a piece of paper one-handed, bracing it against a window sill
videos.notes: tearing paper normally needs two hands; here the sill substitutes for the missing hand to brace the sheet. Visually ambiguous (looks like wiping).
